notes: iOS triage relies on owner-supplied credentials and lawful authority. This toolkit does NOT jailbreak, bypass passcodes, or exploit iOS. The recommended path is an encrypted iTunes/Finder/libimobiledevice backup followed by analysis with MVT iOS or iLEAPP.

tools.name: Apple Configurator 2 / Finder / iTunes
tools.purpose: Native, owner-controlled encrypted backup workflow. Encrypted backups contain richer artifacts than unencrypted ones.
tools.notes: Document the backup encryption password in the case file. Without it, MVT/iLEAPP will be unable to decrypt the backup.

tools.name: DB Browser for SQLite
tools.purpose: View SQLite databases inside an iOS backup (with authority).

tools.name: iLEAPP
tools.purpose: iOS Logs Events And Protobuf Parser — parses iOS backups and filesystem extractions into a report.
tools.notes: Run on the analyst workstation against a decrypted backup or extraction.

tools.name: libimobiledevice
tools.purpose: Open-source library and CLI utilities (idevicebackup2, ideviceinfo, idevicepair, idevice_id) to communicate with iOS devices over USB and create backups.
tools.notes: On macOS use Homebrew (`brew install libimobiledevice`); on Linux use the distro package. The companion script `scripts/collect_ios_backup.sh` calls these utilities.

tools.name: MVT (Mobile Verification Toolkit) — iOS
tools.purpose: Consensual forensic analysis of iOS backups / filesystem dumps (mvt-ios).
tools.notes: Per Amnesty International Security Lab: explicit consent / lawful authority required.
